revision.comment: /* 系譜 */修正
revision.text:
{{複数の問題 |出典の明記=2020-07 |独自研究=2020-07 |特筆性=2020-07 }}
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 遠津年魚眼妙媛 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 遠津年魚目目微比売命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = | 氏族 = | 父母 = 父：[[荒河戸畔]] | 兄弟 = [[久志多麻命]]、[[薩佐奈胡命]] | 子 = [[豊城入彦命]]、[[豊鍬入姫命]] | 特記事項 = }} '''遠津年魚眼妙媛'''（とおつあゆめまぐわしひめ、生没年不詳）は、古代[[日本]]の[[人物]]。
== 系譜 == 父は[[紀国造]]の一族である[[荒河戸畔]]で、[[崇神天皇]]の后となり[[豊城入彦命]]、[[豊鍬入姫命]]を生んだとされる。
== 考証 == 『[[日本書紀]]』などでは[[崇神天皇]]妃となって、所生の皇子[[豊城入彦命]]が異母兄弟の[[垂仁天皇|生目尊]]と皇位継承の比較を行う記事が伝えられる。しかし、[[紀国造]]一族が天皇の后妃に入る慣習はなく、加えて、異母兄弟とされる活目尊の母は皇后の[[御間城姫]]であるから、本来は皇位継承資格を比較されるはずがないと指摘される<ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「二 毛野一族の初期段階ー毛野氏の発生に関する論点」『古代氏族の研究⑰ 毛野氏 東国の雄族諸武家の源流』青垣出版、2021年、31-33頁。</ref>。この説では、[[上毛野国造]]・[[下毛野国造]]一族に[[御諸別命]]の名や[[大神神社|三輪神]]の祭祀が見られること、毛野一族の氏姓が[[三輪氏|三輪君]]一族の氏姓と類似することなどから、毛野一族を三輪君の同族としている。また、崇神天皇妃としての遠津年魚眼眼妙媛を否定しつつ、三輪君と紀国造家が通婚していること、[[那波郡]][[式内社]]の[[火雷神社 (玉村町)|火雷神社]]や[[上野国]]・[[下野国]]に多数分布する[[雷電神社]]など、毛野地域には紀国造の遠祖神である[[雷神]]の祭祀が多数見られることなどから、御諸別命の母系に[[荒河戸畔]]の娘があったと指摘されている<ref>宝賀寿男「二 毛野一族の初期段階ー毛野氏の発生に関する論点」『古代氏族の研究⑰ 毛野氏 東国の雄族諸武家の源流』青垣出版、2021年、55-77頁。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] *[[上毛野氏]] *[[下毛野氏]]
{{デフォルトソート:とおつあゆめまぐわしひめ}} [[Category:紀氏 (神別)]] [[Category:飛鳥時代以前の人物]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 遠津年魚眼眼妙媛 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 遠津年魚目目微比売命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = | 氏族 = | 父母 = 父：[[荒河戸畔]] | 兄弟 = [[久志多麻命]]、[[薩佐奈胡命]] | 子 = [[豊城入彦命]]、[[豊鍬入姫命]] | 特記事項 = }} '''遠津年魚眼眼妙媛'''（とおつあゆめまぐわしひめ、生没年不詳）は、古代[[日本]]の[[人物]]。
== 系譜 == 父は[[紀国造]]の一族である[[荒河戸畔]]で、[[崇神天皇]]の后となり[[豊城入彦命]]・[[豊鍬入姫命]]を生んだとされる。
== 考証 == 『[[日本書紀]]』などでは[[崇神天皇]]妃となって、所生の皇子[[豊城入彦命]]が異母兄弟の[[垂仁天皇|生目尊]]と皇位継承の比較を行う記事が伝えられる。しかし、[[紀国造]]一族が天皇の后妃に入る慣習はなく、加えて、異母兄弟とされる活目尊の母は皇后の[[御間城姫]]であるから、本来は皇位継承資格を比較されるはずがないと指摘される<ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「二 毛野一族の初期段階ー毛野氏の発生に関する論点」『古代氏族の研究⑰ 毛野氏 東国の雄族諸武家の源流』青垣出版、2021年、31-33頁。</ref>。この説では、[[上毛野国造]]・[[下毛野国造]]一族に[[御諸別命]]の名や[[大神神社|三輪神]]の祭祀が見られること、毛野一族の氏姓が[[三輪氏|三輪君]]一族の氏姓と類似することなどから、毛野一族を三輪君の同族としている。また、崇神天皇妃としての遠津年魚眼眼妙媛を否定しつつ、三輪君と紀国造家が通婚していること、[[那波郡]][[式内社]]の[[火雷神社 (玉村町)|火雷神社]]や[[上野国]]・[[下野国]]に多数分布する[[雷電神社]]など、毛野地域には紀国造の遠祖神である[[雷神]]の祭祀が多数見られることなどから、御諸別命の母系に[[荒河戸畔]]の娘があったと指摘されている<ref>宝賀寿男「二 毛野一族の初期段階ー毛野氏の発生に関する論点」『古代氏族の研究⑰ 毛野氏 東国の雄族諸武家の源流』青垣出版、2021年、55-77頁。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble | name = Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period|古墳時代]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Tōtsu Ayume Mememiwashi Hime | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | religious_name = | burial_place = | rank = | allegiance = | clan = | parents = Father: {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔}} | siblings = {{ill|Kushitama no Mikoto|ja|久志多麻命}}, {{ill|Satsunanaho no Mikoto|ja|薩佐奈胡命}} | children = {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, {{ill|Toyosuki Iribime no Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命}} | special_notes = }} '''Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime''' (遠津年魚眼眼妙媛, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient figure in [[Japan]].
== Genealogy ==
Her father was {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔}}, a member of the {{ill|Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造}} clan. She became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, bearing the children {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} and {{ill|Toyosuki Iribime no Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命}}.
== Scholarly Analysis ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other sources, Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime was listed as a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. She bore Prince {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, whose royal succession eligibility was compared to his half-brother, {{ill|Ikume no Mikoto|ja|活目尊}}, later known as {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. However, it has been argued that the {{ill|Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造}} clan traditionally did not intermarry with the imperial family, raising questions about the historicity of this claim.<ref>{{ill|Hōga Toshio|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "The Early Stages of the Keno Clan," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII: The Keno Clan,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 31-33.</ref>
This theory posits that the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|毛野氏}} may have shared ancestry with the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪君}}, evidenced by shared elements in naming conventions and religious practices, such as the worship of {{ill|Miwa deity|ja|三輪神}} and its widespread association with the {{ill|Raiden Shrine|ja|雷電神社}} network in the region. It has been suggested that the maternal lineage of {{ill|Omorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別命}}, a descendant of the Keno clan, included a daughter of Arakawatobime.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "The Role of Keno and Related Clans in Ancient Japanese Society," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 55-77.</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

[[Category:Kofun period figures]] [[Category:Keno clan]] [[Category:Birth and death dates unknown]]
[[ja:遠津年魚眼眼妙媛]]

It has been suggested that the maternal lineage of {{ill|Omorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別命}}, a descendant of the Keno clan, included a daughter of Arakawatobime.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "The Role of Keno and Related Clans in Ancient Japanese Society," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 55-77.</ref>

{{Infobox royalty | name = Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period|古墳時代]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Tōtsu Ayume Mememiwashi Hime | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | religious_name = | burial_place = | rank = | allegiance = | clan = | father= {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔}} | siblings = {{ill|Kushitama no Mikoto|ja|久志多麻命}}, {{ill|Satsunanaho no Mikoto|ja|薩佐奈胡命}} | issue = {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命}} | special_notes = }} '''Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime''' (遠津年魚眼眼妙媛, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient figure in [[Japan]].
== Genealogy ==
Her father was {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔}}, a member of the {{ill|Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造}} clan. She became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, bearing the children {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命}}.
== Scholarly Analysis ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other sources, Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime was listed as a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. She bore Prince {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, whose royal succession eligibility was compared to his half-brother, {{ill|Ikume no Mikoto|ja|活目尊}}, later known as {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. However, it has been argued that the {{ill|Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造}} clan traditionally did not intermarry with the imperial family, raising questions about the historicity of this claim.<ref>{{ill|Hōga Toshio|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "The Early Stages of the Keno Clan," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII: The Keno Clan,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 31-33.</ref>
This theory posits that the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|毛野氏}} may have shared ancestry with the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪君}}, evidenced by shared elements in naming conventions and religious practices, such as the worship of {{ill|Miwa deity|ja|三輪神}} and its widespread association with the {{ill|Raiden Shrine|ja|雷電神社}} network in the region. It has been suggested that the maternal lineage of {{ill|Omorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別命}}, a descendant of the Keno clan, included a daughter of Arakawatobime.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "The Role of Keno and Related Clans in Ancient Japanese Society," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 55-77.</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

{{Infobox royalty | name = Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period|古墳時代]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Tōtsu Ayume Mememiwashi Hime | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | religious_name = | burial_place = | rank = | allegiance = | clan = | father= {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔}} | siblings = {{ill|Kushitama no Mikoto|ja|久志多麻命}}, {{ill|Satsunanaho no Mikoto|ja|薩佐奈胡命}} | issue = {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命}} | special_notes = }} '''Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime''' (遠津年魚眼眼妙媛, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient figure in [[Japan]].
== Genealogy ==
Her father was {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔}}, a member of the {{ill|Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造}} clan. She became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, bearing the children {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命}}.
== Scholarly Analysis ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other sources, Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime was listed as a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. She bore Prince {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, whose royal succession eligibility was compared to his half-brother, Ikume no Mikoto, later known as {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. However, it has been argued that the {{ill|Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造}} clan traditionally did not intermarry with the imperial family, raising questions about the historicity of this claim.<ref>{{ill|Hōga Toshio|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "The Early Stages of the Keno Clan," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII: The Keno Clan,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 31-33.</ref>
This theory posits that the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|毛野氏}} may have shared ancestry with the {{ill|Omononushi|lt=Miwa clan|ja|三輪君}}, evidenced by shared elements in naming conventions and religious practices, such as the worship of {{ill|Miwa deity|ja|三輪神}} and its widespread association with the {{ill|Raiden Shrine|ja|雷電神社}} network in the region. It has been suggested that the maternal lineage of {{ill|Omorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別命}}, a descendant of the Keno clan, included a daughter of Arakawatobime.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "The Role of Keno and Related Clans in Ancient Japanese Society," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 55-77.</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

{{Infobox royalty | name = Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period|古墳時代]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Tōtsu Ayume Mememiwashi Hime | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | religious_name = | burial_place = | rank = | allegiance = | clan = | father= {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔}} | siblings = {{ill|Kushitama no Mikoto|ja|久志多麻命}}, {{ill|Satsunanaho no Mikoto|ja|薩佐奈胡命}} | issue = {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命}} | special_notes = }} '''Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime''' (遠津年魚眼眼妙媛, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient figure in [[Japan]].
== Genealogy ==
Her father was {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔}}, a member of the {{ill|Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造}} clan. She became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, bearing the children {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命}}.
== Scholarly Analysis ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other sources, Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime was listed as a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. She bore Prince {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, whose royal succession eligibility was compared to his half-brother, Ikume no Mikoto, later known as {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. However, it has been argued that the {{ill|Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造}} clan traditionally did not intermarry with the imperial family, raising questions about the historicity of this claim.<ref>{{ill|Hōga Toshio|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "The Early Stages of the Keno Clan," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII: The Keno Clan,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 31-33.</ref>
This theory posits that the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|毛野氏}} may have shared ancestry with the {{ill|Omononushi|lt=Miwa clan|ja|三輪君}}, evidenced by shared elements in naming conventions and religious practices, such as the worship of {{ill|Miwa deity|ja|三輪神}} and its widespread association with the {{ill|Raiden Shrine|ja|雷電神社}} network in the region. It has been suggested that the maternal lineage of {{ill|Omorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別命}}, a descendant of the Keno clan, included a daughter of Arakawatobime.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "The Role of Keno and Related Clans in Ancient Japanese Society," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 55-77.</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[List of people before the Asuka period]] * {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}}

{{Infobox royalty | name = Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period|古墳時代]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Tōtsu Ayume Mememiwashi Hime | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | religious_name = | burial_place = | rank = | allegiance = | clan = | father= {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔}} | siblings = {{ill|Kushitama no Mikoto|ja|久志多麻命}}, {{ill|Satsunanaho no Mikoto|ja|薩佐奈胡命}} | issue = {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命}} | special_notes = }} '''Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime''' (遠津年魚眼眼妙媛, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient figure in [[Japan]].
== Genealogy ==
Her father was {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔}}, a member of the {{ill|Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造}} clan. She became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, bearing the children {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命}}.
== Scholarly Analysis ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other sources, Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime was listed as a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. She bore Prince {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, whose royal succession eligibility was compared to his half-brother, Ikume no Mikoto, later known as {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. However, it has been argued that the {{ill|Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造}} clan traditionally did not intermarry with the imperial family, raising questions about the historicity of this claim.<ref>{{ill|Hōga Toshio|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "The Early Stages of the Keno Clan," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII: The Keno Clan,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 31-33.</ref>
This theory posits that the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|毛野氏}} may have shared ancestry with the {{ill|Omononushi|lt=Miwa clan|ja|三輪君}}, evidenced by shared elements in naming conventions and religious practices, such as the worship of {{ill|Miwa deity|ja|三輪神}} and its widespread association with the {{ill|Raiden Shrine|ja|雷電神社}} network in the region. It has been suggested that the maternal lineage of {{ill|Omorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別命}}, a descendant of the Keno clan, included a daughter of Arakawatobime.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "The Role of Keno and Related Clans in Ancient Japanese Society," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 55-77.</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox royalty | name = Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period|古墳時代]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Tōtsu Ayume Mememiwashi Hime | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | religious_name = | burial_place = | rank = | allegiance = | clan = | father= {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔}} | siblings = {{ill|Kushitama no Mikoto|ja|久志多麻命}}, {{ill|Satsunanaho no Mikoto|ja|薩佐奈胡命}} | issue = {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命}} | special_notes = }} '''Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime''' (遠津年魚眼眼妙媛, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient figure in [[Japan]].
== Genealogy ==
Her father was {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔}}, a member of the {{ill|Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造}} clan. She became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, bearing the children {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命}}.
== Scholarly Analysis ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other sources, Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime was listed as a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. She bore Prince {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, whose royal succession eligibility was compared to his half-brother, Ikume no Mikoto, later known as {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. However, it has been argued that the {{ill|Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造}} clan traditionally did not intermarry with the imperial family, raising questions about the historicity of this claim.<ref>{{ill|Hōga Toshio|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "The Early Stages of the Keno Clan," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII: The Keno Clan,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 31-33.</ref>
This theory posits that the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|毛野氏}} may have shared ancestry with the {{ill|Omononushi|lt=Miwa clan|ja|三輪君}}, evidenced by shared elements in naming conventions and religious practices, such as the worship of {{ill|Miwa deity|ja|三輪神}} and its widespread association with the {{ill|Raiden Shrine|ja|雷電神社}} network in the region. It has been suggested that the maternal lineage of {{ill|Omorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別命}}, a descendant of the Keno clan, included a daughter of Arakawatobime.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "The Role of Keno and Related Clans in Ancient Japanese Society," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 55-77.</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[List of people before the Asuka period]] * {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}}

However, it has been argued that the {{ill|Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造}} clan traditionally did not intermarry with the imperial family, raising questions about the historicity of this claim.<ref>{{ill|Hōga Toshio|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "The Early Stages of the Keno Clan," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII: The Keno Clan,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 31-33.</ref>
This theory posits that the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|毛野氏}} may have shared ancestry with the {{ill|Omononushi|lt=Miwa clan|ja|三輪君}}, evidenced by shared elements in naming conventions and religious practices, such as the worship of {{ill|Miwa deity|ja|三輪神}} and its widespread association with the {{ill|Raiden Shrine|ja|雷電神社}} network in the region. It has been suggested that the maternal lineage of {{ill|Omorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別命}}, a descendant of the Keno clan, included a daughter of Arakawatobime.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "The Role of Keno and Related Clans in Ancient Japanese Society," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 55-77.</ref>

{{Infobox royalty | name = Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period|古墳時代]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Tōtsu Ayume Mememiwashi Hime | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | religious_name = | burial_place = | rank = | allegiance = | clan = | father= {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔}} | siblings = {{ill|Kushitama no Mikoto|ja|久志多麻命}}, {{ill|Satsunanaho no Mikoto|ja|薩佐奈胡命}} | issue = {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命}} | special_notes = }} '''Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime''' (遠津年魚眼眼妙媛, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient figure in [[Japan]].
== Genealogy ==
Her father was {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔}}, a member of the {{ill|Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造}} clan. She became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, bearing the children {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命}}.
== Scholarly Analysis ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other sources, Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime was listed as a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. She bore Prince {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, whose royal succession eligibility was compared to his half-brother, Ikume no Mikoto, later known as {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. However, it has been argued that the {{ill|Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造}} clan traditionally did not intermarry with the imperial family, raising questions about the historicity of this claim.<ref>{{ill|Hōga Toshio|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "The Early Stages of the Keno Clan," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII: The Keno Clan,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 31-33.</ref>
This theory posits that the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|毛野氏}} may have shared ancestry with the {{ill|Omononushi|lt=Miwa clan|ja|三輪君}}, evidenced by shared elements in naming conventions and religious practices, such as the worship of {{ill|Miwa deity|ja|三輪神}} and its widespread association with the {{ill|Raiden Shrine|ja|雷電神社}} network in the region. It has been suggested that the maternal lineage of {{ill|Omorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別命}}, a descendant of the Keno clan, included a daughter of Arakawatobime.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "The Role of Keno and Related Clans in Ancient Japanese Society," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 55-77.</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[List of people before the Asuka period]] * {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}}

Her father was {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔}}, a member of the {{ill|Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造}} clan. She became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, bearing the children {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命}}.

According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other sources, Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime was listed as a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. She bore Prince {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, whose royal succession eligibility was compared to his half-brother, Ikume no Mikoto, later known as {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. However, it has been argued that the {{ill|Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造}} clan traditionally did not intermarry with the imperial family, raising questions about the historicity of this claim.<ref>{{ill|Hōga Toshio|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "The Early Stages of the Keno Clan," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII: The Keno Clan,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 31-33.</ref>
This theory posits that the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|毛野氏}} may have shared ancestry with the {{ill|Omononushi|lt=Miwa clan|ja|三輪君}}, evidenced by shared elements in naming conventions and religious practices, such as the worship of {{ill|Miwa deity|ja|三輪神}} and its widespread association with the {{ill|Raiden Shrine|ja|雷電神社}} network in the region. It has been suggested that the maternal lineage of {{ill|Omorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別命}}, a descendant of the Keno clan, included a daughter of Arakawatobime.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "The Role of Keno and Related Clans in Ancient Japanese Society," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 55-77.</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

She bore Prince {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, whose royal succession eligibility was compared to his half-brother, Ikume no Mikoto, later known as {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. However, it has been argued that the {{ill|Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造}} clan traditionally did not intermarry with the imperial family, raising questions about the historicity of this claim.<ref>{{ill|Hōga Toshio|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "The Early Stages of the Keno Clan," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII: The Keno Clan,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 31-33.</ref>
This theory posits that the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|毛野氏}} may have shared ancestry with the {{ill|Omononushi|lt=Miwa clan|ja|三輪君}}, evidenced by shared elements in naming conventions and religious practices, such as the worship of {{ill|Miwa deity|ja|三輪神}} and its widespread association with the {{ill|Raiden Shrine|ja|雷電神社}} network in the region. It has been suggested that the maternal lineage of {{ill|Omorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別命}}, a descendant of the Keno clan, included a daughter of Arakawatobime.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "The Role of Keno and Related Clans in Ancient Japanese Society," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 55-77.</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

Her father was {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔}}, a member of the {{ill|Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造}} clan. She became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, bearing the children {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}}.
== Scholarly Analysis ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other sources, Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime was listed as a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. She bore Prince {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, whose royal succession eligibility was compared to his half-brother, Ikume no Mikoto, later known as {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. However, it has been argued that the {{ill|Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造}} clan traditionally did not intermarry with the imperial family, raising questions about the historicity of this claim.<ref>{{ill|Hōga Toshio|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "The Early Stages of the Keno Clan," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII: The Keno Clan,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 31-33.</ref>
This theory posits that the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|毛野氏}} may have shared ancestry with the {{ill|Omononushi|lt=Miwa clan|ja|三輪君}}, evidenced by shared elements in naming conventions and religious practices, such as the worship of {{ill|Miwa deity|ja|三輪神}} and its widespread association with the {{ill|Raiden Shrine|ja|雷電神社}} network in the region. It has been suggested that the maternal lineage of {{ill|Omorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別命|1=Mimorowake|lt=Mimorowake-no-Mikoto}}, a descendant of the Keno clan, included a daughter of Arakawatobime.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "The Role of Keno and Related Clans in Ancient Japanese Society," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 55-77.</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[List of people before the Asuka period]] * {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox royalty | name = Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period|古墳時代]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Tōtsu Ayume Mememiwashi Hime | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | religious_name = | burial_place = | rank = | allegiance = | clan = | father= {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔}} | siblings = {{ill|Kushitama no Mikoto|ja|久志多麻命}}, {{ill|Satsunanaho no Mikoto|ja|薩佐奈胡命}} | issue = {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}} | special_notes = }} '''Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime''' (遠津年魚眼眼妙媛, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient figure in [[Japan]].
== Genealogy ==
Her father was {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔}}, a member of the {{ill|Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造}} clan. She became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, bearing the children {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}}.
== Scholarly Analysis ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other sources, Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime was listed as a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. She bore Prince {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, whose royal succession eligibility was compared to his half-brother, Ikume no Mikoto, later known as {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. However, it has been argued that the {{ill|Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造}} clan traditionally did not intermarry with the imperial family, raising questions about the historicity of this claim.<ref>{{ill|Hōga Toshio|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "The Early Stages of the Keno Clan," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII: The Keno Clan,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 31-33.</ref>
This theory posits that the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|毛野氏}} may have shared ancestry with the {{ill|Omononushi|lt=Miwa clan|ja|三輪君}}, evidenced by shared elements in naming conventions and religious practices, such as the worship of {{ill|Miwa deity|ja|三輪神}} and its widespread association with the {{ill|Raiden Shrine|ja|雷電神社}} network in the region. It has been suggested that the maternal lineage of {{ill|Omorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別命|1=Mimorowake|lt=Mimorowake-no-Mikoto}}, a descendant of the Keno clan, included a daughter of Arakawatobime.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "The Role of Keno and Related Clans in Ancient Japanese Society," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 55-77.</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[List of people before the Asuka period]] * {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox royalty | name = Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period|古墳時代]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Tōtsu Ayume Mememiwashi Hime | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | religious_name = | burial_place = | rank = | allegiance = | clan = | father= {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔}} | siblings = {{ill|Kushitama no Mikoto|ja|久志多麻命}}, {{ill|Satsunanaho no Mikoto|ja|薩佐奈胡命}} | issue = {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}} | special_notes = }} '''Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime''' (遠津年魚眼眼妙媛, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient figure in [[Japan]].
== Genealogy ==
Her father was {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔}}, a member of the {{ill|Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造}} clan. She became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, bearing the children {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}}.
== Scholarly Analysis ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other sources, Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime was listed as a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. She bore Prince {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, whose royal succession eligibility was compared to his half-brother, Ikume no Mikoto, later known as {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. However, it has been argued that the {{ill|Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造}} clan traditionally did not intermarry with the imperial family, raising questions about the historicity of this claim.<ref>{{ill|Hōga Toshio|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "The Early Stages of the Keno Clan," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII: The Keno Clan,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 31-33.</ref>
This theory posits that the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|毛野氏}} may have shared ancestry with the {{ill|Omononushi|lt=Miwa clan|ja|三輪君}}, evidenced by shared elements in naming conventions and religious practices, such as the worship of {{ill|Miwa deity|ja|三輪神}} and its widespread association with the {{ill|Raiden Shrine|ja|雷電神社}} network in the region. It has been suggested that the maternal lineage of {{ill|Omorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別命|1=Mimorowake|lt=Mimorowake-no-Mikoto}}, a descendant of the Keno clan, included a daughter of Arakawatobime.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "The Role of Keno and Related Clans in Ancient Japanese Society," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 55-77.</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[List of people before the Asuka period]] * {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}

{{Infobox royalty | name = Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period|古墳時代]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Tōtsu Ayume Mememiwashi Hime | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | religious_name = | burial_place = | rank = | allegiance = | clan = | father= {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔}} | siblings = {{ill|Kushitama no Mikoto|ja|久志多麻命}}, {{ill|Satsunanaho no Mikoto|ja|薩佐奈胡命}} | issue = {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}} | special_notes = }} '''Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime''' (遠津年魚眼眼妙媛, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient figure in [[Japan]].

{{Infobox royalty | name = Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period|古墳時代]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Tōtsu Ayume Mememiwashi Hime | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | religious_name = | burial_place = | rank = | allegiance = | clan = | father= {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔}} | siblings = {{ill|Kushitama no Mikoto|ja|久志多麻命}}, {{ill|Satsunanaho no Mikoto|ja|薩佐奈胡命}} | issue = {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}} | special_notes = }} '''Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime''' (遠津年魚眼眼妙媛, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient figure in [[Japan]].
== Genealogy ==
Her father was {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔}}, a member of the {{ill|Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造}} clan. She became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, bearing the children {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}}.
== Scholarly Analysis ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other sources, Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime was listed as a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. She bore Prince {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, whose royal succession eligibility was compared to his half-brother, Ikume no Mikoto, later known as {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. However, it has been argued that the {{ill|Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造}} clan traditionally did not intermarry with the imperial family, raising questions about the historicity of this claim.<ref>{{ill|Hōga Toshio|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "The Early Stages of the Keno Clan," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII: The Keno Clan,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 31-33.</ref>
This theory posits that the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|毛野氏}} may have shared ancestry with the {{ill|Omononushi|lt=Miwa clan|ja|三輪君}}, evidenced by shared elements in naming conventions and religious practices, such as the worship of {{ill|Miwa deity|ja|三輪神}} and its widespread association with the {{ill|Raiden Shrine|ja|雷電神社}} network in the region. It has been suggested that the maternal lineage of {{ill|Omorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別命|1=Mimorowake|lt=Mimorowake-no-Mikoto}}, a descendant of the Keno clan, included a daughter of Arakawatobime.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "The Role of Keno and Related Clans in Ancient Japanese Society," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 55-77.</ref>

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox royalty | name = Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period|古墳時代]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Tōtsu Ayume Mememiwashi Hime | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | religious_name = | burial_place = | rank = | allegiance = | clan = | father= {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔}} | siblings = {{ill|Kushitama no Mikoto|ja|久志多麻命}}, {{ill|Satsunanaho no Mikoto|ja|薩佐奈胡命}} | issue = {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}} | special_notes = }} '''Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime''' (遠津年魚眼眼妙媛, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient figure in [[Japan]].
== Genealogy ==
Her father was {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔}}, a member of the {{ill|Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造}} clan. She became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, bearing the children {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}}.
== Scholarly Analysis ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other sources, Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime was listed as a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. She bore Prince {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, whose royal succession eligibility was compared to his half-brother, Ikume no Mikoto, later known as {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. However, it has been argued that the {{ill|Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造}} clan traditionally did not intermarry with the imperial family, raising questions about the historicity of this claim.<ref>{{ill|Hōga Toshio|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "The Early Stages of the Keno Clan," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII: The Keno Clan,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 31-33.</ref>
This theory posits that the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|毛野氏}} may have shared ancestry with the {{ill|Omononushi|lt=Miwa clan|ja|三輪君}}, evidenced by shared elements in naming conventions and religious practices, such as the worship of {{ill|Miwa deity|ja|三輪神}} and its widespread association with the {{ill|Raiden Shrine|ja|雷電神社}} network in the region. It has been suggested that the maternal lineage of {{ill|Omorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別命|1=Mimorowake|lt=Mimorowake-no-Mikoto}}, a descendant of the Keno clan, included a daughter of Arakawatobime.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "The Role of Keno and Related Clans in Ancient Japanese Society," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 55-77.</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[List of people before the Asuka period]] * {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox royalty | name = Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period|古墳時代]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Tōtsu Ayume Mememiwashi Hime | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | religious_name = | burial_place = | rank = | allegiance = | clan = | father= {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔}} | siblings = {{ill|Kushitama no Mikoto|ja|久志多麻命}}, {{ill|Satsunanaho no Mikoto|ja|薩佐奈胡命}} | issue = {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}} | special_notes = }} '''Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime''' (遠津年魚眼眼妙媛, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient figure in [[Japan]].
== Genealogy ==
Her father was {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔}}, a member of the {{ill|Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造}} clan. She became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, bearing the children {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}}.
== Scholarly Analysis ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and other sources, Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime was listed as a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. She bore Prince {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, whose royal succession eligibility was compared to his half-brother, Ikume no Mikoto, later known as {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}. However, it has been argued that the {{ill|Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造}} clan traditionally did not intermarry with the imperial family, raising questions about the historicity of this claim.<ref>{{ill|Hōga Toshio|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "The Early Stages of the Keno Clan," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII: The Keno Clan,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 31-33.</ref>
This theory posits that the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|毛野氏}} may have shared ancestry with the {{ill|Omononushi|lt=Miwa clan|ja|三輪君}}, evidenced by shared elements in naming conventions and religious practices, such as the worship of {{ill|Miwa deity|ja|三輪神}} and its widespread association with the {{ill|Raiden Shrine|ja|雷電神社}} network in the region. It has been suggested that the maternal lineage of {{ill|Omorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別命|1=Mimorowake|lt=Mimorowake-no-Mikoto}}, a descendant of the Keno clan, included a daughter of Arakawatobime.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "The Role of Keno and Related Clans in Ancient Japanese Society," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 55-77.</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of people before the Asuka period]] * {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}

It has been suggested that the maternal lineage of {{ill|Omorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別命|1=Mimorowake|lt=Mimorowake-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}, a descendant of the Keno clan, included a daughter of Arakawatobime.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "The Role of Keno and Related Clans in Ancient Japanese Society," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 55-77.</ref>

revision.text:
{{Infobox royalty | name = Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period|古墳時代]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Tōtsu Ayume Mememiwashi Hime | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | religious_name = | burial_place = | rank = | allegiance = | clan = | father= {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔|12=draft}} | siblings = {{ill|Kushitama no Mikoto|ja|久志多麻命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Satsunanaho no Mikoto|ja|薩佐奈胡命|12=draft}} | issue = {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|12=draft}} | special_notes = }} '''Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime''' (遠津年魚眼眼妙媛, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient figure in [[Japan]].
== Genealogy ==
Her father was {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔|12=draft}}, a member of the {{ill|Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|12=draft}} clan. She became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}}, bearing the children {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|12=draft}}.
== Scholarly Analysis ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} and other sources, Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime was listed as a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}}. She bore Prince {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}, whose royal succession eligibility was compared to his half-brother, Ikume no Mikoto, later known as {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|12=draft}}. However, it has been argued that the {{ill|Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|12=draft}} clan traditionally did not intermarry with the imperial family, raising questions about the historicity of this claim.<ref>{{ill|Hōga Toshio|ja|宝賀寿男|12=draft}}, "The Early Stages of the Keno Clan," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII: The Keno Clan,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 31-33.</ref>
This theory posits that the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|毛野氏|12=draft}} may have shared ancestry with the {{ill|Omononushi|lt=Miwa clan|ja|三輪君|12=draft}}, evidenced by shared elements in naming conventions and religious practices, such as the worship of {{ill|Miwa deity|ja|三輪神|12=draft}} and its widespread association with the {{ill|Raiden Shrine|ja|雷電神社|12=draft}} network in the region. It has been suggested that the maternal lineage of {{ill|Omorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別命|1=Mimorowake|lt=Mimorowake-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}, a descendant of the Keno clan, included a daughter of Arakawatobime.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "The Role of Keno and Related Clans in Ancient Japanese Society," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 55-77.</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of people before the Asuka period]] * {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=draft}} <!--Totsu Ayumemaguwashihime-->

12 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:遠津年魚眼眼妙媛 up to rev 88003226
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 遠津年魚眼眼妙媛 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 遠津年魚目目微比売命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = | 氏族 = | 父母 = 父：[[荒河戸畔]] | 兄弟 = [[久志多麻命]]、[[薩佐奈胡命]] | 子 = [[豊城入彦命]]、[[豊鍬入姫命]] | 特記事項 = }} '''遠津年魚眼眼妙媛'''（とおつあゆめまぐわしひめ、生没年不詳）は、古代[[日本]]の[[人物]]。
== 系譜 == 父は[[紀国造]]の一族である[[荒河戸畔]]で、[[崇神天皇]]の后となり[[豊城入彦命]]・[[豊鍬入姫命]]を生んだとされる。
== 考証 == 『[[日本書紀]]』などでは[[崇神天皇]]妃となって、所生の皇子[[豊城入彦命]]が異母兄弟の[[垂仁天皇|生目尊]]と皇位継承の比較を行う記事が伝えられる。しかし、[[紀国造]]一族が天皇の后妃に入る慣習はなく、加えて、異母兄弟とされる活目尊の母は皇后の[[御間城姫]]であるから、本来は皇位継承資格を比較されるはずがないと指摘される<ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「二 毛野一族の初期段階ー毛野氏の発生に関する論点」『古代氏族の研究⑰ 毛野氏 東国の雄族諸武家の源流』青垣出版、2021年、31-33頁。</ref>。この説では、[[上毛野国造]]・[[下毛野国造]]一族に[[御諸別命]]の名や[[大神神社|三輪神]]の祭祀が見られること、毛野一族の氏姓が[[三輪氏|三輪君]]一族の氏姓と類似することなどから、毛野一族を三輪君の同族としている。また、崇神天皇妃としての遠津年魚眼眼妙媛を否定しつつ、三輪君と紀国造家が通婚していること、[[那波郡]][[式内社]]の[[火雷神社 (玉村町)|火雷神社]]や[[上野国]]・[[下野国]]に多数分布する[[雷電神社]]など、毛野地域には紀国造の遠祖神である[[雷神]]の祭祀が多数見られることなどから、御諸別命の母系に[[荒河戸畔]]の娘があったと指摘されている<ref>宝賀寿男「二 毛野一族の初期段階ー毛野氏の発生に関する論点」『古代氏族の研究⑰ 毛野氏 東国の雄族諸武家の源流』青垣出版、2021年、55-77頁。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] *[[上毛野氏]] *[[下毛野氏]]
{{デフォルトソート:とおつあゆめまくわしひめ}} [[Category:紀氏 (神別)]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox royalty | name = Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period|古墳時代]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Tōtsu Ayume Mememiwashi Hime | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | religious_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | allegiance = | clan = | father= {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | siblings = {{ill|Kushitama no Mikoto|ja|久志多麻命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Satsunanaho no Mikoto|ja|薩佐奈胡命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | issue = {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | special_notes = }} '''Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime''' (遠津年魚眼眼妙媛, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient figure in [[Japan]].
== Genealogy ==
Her father was {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a member of the {{ill|Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} clan. She became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, bearing the children {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Scholarly Analysis ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and other sources, Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime was listed as a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. She bore Prince {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, whose royal succession eligibility was compared to his half-brother, Ikume no Mikoto, later known as {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, it has been argued that the {{ill|Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} clan traditionally did not intermarry with the imperial family, raising questions about the historicity of this claim.<ref>{{ill|Hōga Toshio|ja|宝賀寿男|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "The Early Stages of the Keno Clan," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII: The Keno Clan,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 31-33.</ref>
This theory posits that the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|毛野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} may have shared ancestry with the {{ill|Omononushi|lt=Miwa clan|ja|三輪君|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, evidenced by shared elements in naming conventions and religious practices, such as the worship of {{ill|Miwa deity|ja|三輪神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and its widespread association with the {{ill|Raiden Shrine|ja|雷電神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} network in the region. It has been suggested that the maternal lineage of {{ill|Omorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別命|1=Mimorowake|lt=Mimorowake-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a descendant of the Keno clan, included a daughter of Arakawatobime.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "The Role of Keno and Related Clans in Ancient Japanese Society," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 55-77.</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of people before the Asuka period]] * {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{translated page|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛|version=88003226|comment=Imported full ja history}}

== Genealogy ==
Her father was {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a member of the {{ill|Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} clan. She became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, bearing the children {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Scholarly Analysis ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and other sources, Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime was listed as a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. She bore Prince {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, whose royal succession eligibility was compared to his half-brother, Ikume no Mikoto, later known as {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, it has been argued that the {{ill|Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} clan traditionally did not intermarry with the imperial family, raising questions about the historicity of this claim.<ref>{{ill|Hōga Toshio|ja|宝賀寿男|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "The Early Stages of the Keno Clan," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII: The Keno Clan,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 31-33.</ref>
This theory posits that the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|毛野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} may have shared ancestry with the {{ill|Omononushi|lt=Miwa clan|ja|三輪君|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, evidenced by shared elements in naming conventions and religious practices, such as the worship of {{ill|Miwa deity|ja|三輪神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and its widespread association with the {{ill|Raiden Shrine|ja|雷電神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} network in the region. It has been suggested that the maternal lineage of {{ill|Omorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別命|1=Mimorowake|lt=Mimorowake-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a descendant of the Keno clan, included a daughter of Arakawatobime.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "The Role of Keno and Related Clans in Ancient Japanese Society," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 55-77.</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of people before the Asuka period]] * {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

However, it has been argued that the {{ill|Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikoku no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan traditionally did not intermarry with the imperial family, raising questions about the historicity of this claim.<ref>{{ill|Hōga Toshio|ja|宝賀寿男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōga Toshio|qq=}}, "The Early Stages of the Keno Clan," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII: The Keno Clan,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 31-33.</ref>

It has been suggested that the maternal lineage of {{ill|Omorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別命|1=Mimorowake|lt=Mimorowake-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omorowake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}, a descendant of the Keno clan, included a daughter of Arakawatobime.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "The Role of Keno and Related Clans in Ancient Japanese Society," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 55-77.</ref>

==Merged second translation== {{Infobox noble | name = Tōtsu Ayumemaguwashi Hime | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | birth_name = | other_names = Tōtsu Ayumememawashihime no Mikoto | posthumous_name = | deity_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_Rank = | allegiance = | family = | parents = Father: {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawatobime|qq=}} | siblings = [[Kushitama no Mikoto]], [[Satsusana no Mikoto]] | children = [[Toyokuni Iribiko no Mikoto]], [[Toyokuniiri Hime no Mikoto]] | notes = | Name = Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi-hime | image = | image size = | Caption = | Era = {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} | Birth_date = Unknown | Death_date = Unknown | Renamed = | Other names = Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi-hime no Mikoto | Posthumous name = | Divine name = | Buddhist name = | Burial_place = | Court_Rank = | Master = | Clan = | Parents = Father: {{ill|Arakawato Hime|ja|荒河戸畔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawato Hime|qq=}} | Siblings = {{ill|Kushitama no Mikoto|ja|久志多麻命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushitama no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Sasazano no Mikoto|ja|薩佐奈胡命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasazano no Mikoto|qq=}} | Children = {{ill|Toyokiiri Hiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiri Hiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyokumori Hime no Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumori Hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}} | Notes = }}
'''Tōtsu Ayumemaguwashi Hime''' (遠津年魚眼眼妙媛, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient Japanese figure from the [[Kofun period]]. '''Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi-hime''' (遠津年魚眼眼妙媛, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient {{ill|Japan|ja|日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan|qq=}}ese figure from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}.
== Genealogy == She was the daughter of {{ill|Arakawato Hime|ja|荒河戸畔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawato Hime|qq=}}, a member of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. She became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}} and gave birth to {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}.
== Historical Analysis == In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} and other sources, Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi-hime is described as a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}, whose son, {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, is compared with his half-brother {{ill|Ikume Iribiko Isachi|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikume Iribiko Isachi|qq=}} in discussions of imperial succession. However, scholars note that it was not customary for women of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} to enter the emperor's harem. Additionally, Ikume no Mikoto's mother, {{ill|Mimaki-hime|ja|御間城姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimaki-hime|qq=}}, was an empress, making such a comparison unlikely.<ref>{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshio Hōga|qq=}}, "2. The Early Stages of the Keno Clan: Issues Surrounding the Emergence of the Keno Lineage," in *Studies of Ancient Clans Vol. 17: The Keno Clan – The Origins of the Eastern Province's Prominent Lineage*, Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 31–33.</ref>
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, Tōtsu Ayumemaguwashi Hime was one of the consorts of [[Emperor Sujin]]. She is credited with giving birth to {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, who is mentioned in a narrative comparing his potential for succession against his half-brother, {{ill|Ikume Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|活目尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikume Iribiko no Mikoto|qq=}} (later [[Emperor Suinin]]).
However, scholars question the credibility of this account. It has been noted that the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan did not traditionally intermarry with the imperial family, and succession comparisons with Emperor Suinin, whose mother was Empress {{ill|Mimaki-hime|ja|御間城姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimaki-hime|qq=}}, would not have been appropriate.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Kōdai Shizoku no Kenkyū XVII: Keno-shi Higashi no Yūzoku Shobuke no Genryū|author=Toshio Hōga|publisher=Aogaki Shuppan|year=2021|pages=31–33}}</ref>
Some scholars argue that the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|毛野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|qq=}}, which included the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, was closely related to the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa clan|qq=}}. The {{ill|Miwa deity|ja|三輪神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa deity|qq=}} and associated rituals are prominent within the Keno clan's lineage, and similarities in surnames suggest a shared ancestry.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Kōdai Shizoku no Kenkyū XVII: Keno-shi Higashi no Yūzoku Shobuke no Genryū|author=Toshio Hōga|publisher=Aogaki Shuppan|year=2021|pages=55–77}}</ref>
Despite doubts about her role as an imperial consort, connections between the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa clan|qq=}} have been suggested, particularly through marriage alliances. Additionally, the presence of numerous shrines dedicated to {{ill|Raijin|ja|雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|qq=}}—a deity associated with the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|qq=}}—in regions linked to the Keno clan further supports the hypothesis that Tōtsu Ayumemaguwashi Hime may have maternal ties to these groups.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Kōdai Shizoku no Kenkyū XVII: Keno-shi Higashi no Yūzoku Shobuke no Genryū|author=Toshio Hōga|publisher=Aogaki Shuppan|year=2021|pages=55–77}}</ref>
{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshio Hōga|qq=}} argues that the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|毛野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|qq=}} (later associated with the provinces of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzuke Province|qq=}} and {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Province|qq=}}) may have been related to the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa clan|qq=}}, citing similarities in clan surnames and evidence of {{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|大神神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine|qq=}} worship among the Keno lineage. He also suggests that while Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi-hime may not have been a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}, it is plausible that her lineage connected with the maternal ancestry of {{ill|Mimorowake|ja|御諸別命|Mimorowake=Mimorowake|lt=Omimorowake no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}, who was linked to both the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|毛野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ki no Kuni clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. Supporting evidence includes the prominence of thunder deity worship in the Keno regions, such as at {{ill|Kaminari Shrines|ja|雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminari Shrines|qq=}}.<ref>{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshio Hōga|qq=}}, "2. The Early Stages of the Keno Clan: Issues Surrounding the Emergence of the Keno Lineage," in *Studies of Ancient Clans Vol. 17: The Keno Clan – The Origins of the Eastern Province's Prominent Lineage*, Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 55–77.</ref>
== See also == * {{ill|List of figures before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of figures before the Asuka period|qq=}} * [[Kamitsukeno clan]] * [[Shimotsukeno clan]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox royalty | name = Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period|古墳時代]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Tōtsu Ayume Mememiwashi Hime | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | religious_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | allegiance = | clan = | father= {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawatobime|qq=}} | siblings = {{ill|Kushitama no Mikoto|ja|久志多麻命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushitama no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Satsunanaho no Mikoto|ja|薩佐奈胡命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satsunanaho no Mikoto|qq=}} | issue = {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}} | special_notes = }} '''Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime''' (遠津年魚眼眼妙媛, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient figure in [[Japan]].
== Genealogy ==
Her father was {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawatobime|qq=}}, a member of the {{ill|Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikoku no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan. She became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}, bearing the children {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}.
== Scholarly Analysis ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} and other sources, Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime was listed as a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}. She bore Prince {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, whose royal succession eligibility was compared to his half-brother, Ikume no Mikoto, later known as {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Suinin|qq=}}. However, it has been argued that the {{ill|Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikoku no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan traditionally did not intermarry with the imperial family, raising questions about the historicity of this claim.<ref>{{ill|Hōga Toshio|ja|宝賀寿男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōga Toshio|qq=}}, "The Early Stages of the Keno Clan," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII: The Keno Clan,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 31-33.</ref>
This theory posits that the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|毛野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|qq=}} may have shared ancestry with the {{ill|Omononushi|lt=Miwa clan|ja|三輪君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omononushi|qq=}}, evidenced by shared elements in naming conventions and religious practices, such as the worship of {{ill|Miwa deity|ja|三輪神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa deity|qq=}} and its widespread association with the {{ill|Raiden Shrine|ja|雷電神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raiden Shrine|qq=}} network in the region. It has been suggested that the maternal lineage of {{ill|Omorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別命|1=Mimorowake|lt=Mimorowake-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omorowake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}, a descendant of the Keno clan, included a daughter of Arakawatobime.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "The Role of Keno and Related Clans in Ancient Japanese Society," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 55-77.</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of people before the Asuka period]] * {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}

==Merged second translation== {{Infobox noble | name = Tōtsu Ayumemaguwashi Hime | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | birth_name = | other_names = Tōtsu Ayumememawashihime no Mikoto | posthumous_name = | deity_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_Rank = | allegiance = | family = | parents = Father: {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawatobime|qq=}} | siblings = [[Kushitama no Mikoto]], [[Satsusana no Mikoto]] | children = [[Toyokuni Iribiko no Mikoto]], [[Toyokuniiri Hime no Mikoto]] | notes = | Name = Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi-hime | image = | image size = | Caption = | Era = {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} | Birth_date = Unknown | Death_date = Unknown | Renamed = | Other names = Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi-hime no Mikoto | Posthumous name = | Divine name = | Buddhist name = | Burial_place = | Court_Rank = | Master = | Clan = | Parents = Father: {{ill|Arakawato Hime|ja|荒河戸畔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawato Hime|qq=}} | Siblings = {{ill|Kushitama no Mikoto|ja|久志多麻命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushitama no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Sasazano no Mikoto|ja|薩佐奈胡命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasazano no Mikoto|qq=}} | Children = {{ill|Toyokiiri Hiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiri Hiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyokumori Hime no Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumori Hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}} | Notes = }}
'''Tōtsu Ayumemaguwashi Hime''' (遠津年魚眼眼妙媛, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient Japanese figure from the [[Kofun period]]. '''Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi-hime''' (遠津年魚眼眼妙媛, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient {{ill|Japan|ja|日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan|qq=}}ese figure from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}.
== Genealogy == She was the daughter of {{ill|Arakawato Hime|ja|荒河戸畔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawato Hime|qq=}}, a member of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. She became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}} and gave birth to {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}.
== Historical Analysis == In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} and other sources, Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi-hime is described as a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}, whose son, {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, is compared with his half-brother {{ill|Ikume Iribiko Isachi|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikume Iribiko Isachi|qq=}} in discussions of imperial succession. However, scholars note that it was not customary for women of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} to enter the emperor's harem. Additionally, Ikume no Mikoto's mother, {{ill|Mimaki-hime|ja|御間城姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimaki-hime|qq=}}, was an empress, making such a comparison unlikely.<ref>{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshio Hōga|qq=}}, "2. The Early Stages of the Keno Clan: Issues Surrounding the Emergence of the Keno Lineage," in *Studies of Ancient Clans Vol. 17: The Keno Clan – The Origins of the Eastern Province's Prominent Lineage*, Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 31–33.</ref>
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, Tōtsu Ayumemaguwashi Hime was one of the consorts of [[Emperor Sujin]]. She is credited with giving birth to {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, who is mentioned in a narrative comparing his potential for succession against his half-brother, {{ill|Ikume Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|活目尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikume Iribiko no Mikoto|qq=}} (later [[Emperor Suinin]]).
However, scholars question the credibility of this account. It has been noted that the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan did not traditionally intermarry with the imperial family, and succession comparisons with Emperor Suinin, whose mother was Empress {{ill|Mimaki-hime|ja|御間城姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimaki-hime|qq=}}, would not have been appropriate.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Kōdai Shizoku no Kenkyū XVII: Keno-shi Higashi no Yūzoku Shobuke no Genryū|author=Toshio Hōga|publisher=Aogaki Shuppan|year=2021|pages=31–33}}</ref>
Some scholars argue that the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|毛野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|qq=}}, which included the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, was closely related to the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa clan|qq=}}. The {{ill|Miwa deity|ja|三輪神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa deity|qq=}} and associated rituals are prominent within the Keno clan's lineage, and similarities in surnames suggest a shared ancestry.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Kōdai Shizoku no Kenkyū XVII: Keno-shi Higashi no Yūzoku Shobuke no Genryū|author=Toshio Hōga|publisher=Aogaki Shuppan|year=2021|pages=55–77}}</ref>
Despite doubts about her role as an imperial consort, connections between the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa clan|qq=}} have been suggested, particularly through marriage alliances. Additionally, the presence of numerous shrines dedicated to {{ill|Raijin|ja|雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|qq=}}—a deity associated with the {{ill|Ki clan|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|qq=}}—in regions linked to the Keno clan further supports the hypothesis that Tōtsu Ayumemaguwashi Hime may have maternal ties to these groups.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Kōdai Shizoku no Kenkyū XVII: Keno-shi Higashi no Yūzoku Shobuke no Genryū|author=Toshio Hōga|publisher=Aogaki Shuppan|year=2021|pages=55–77}}</ref>
{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshio Hōga|qq=}} argues that the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|毛野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|qq=}} (later associated with the provinces of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzuke Province|qq=}} and {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Province|qq=}}) may have been related to the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa clan|qq=}}, citing similarities in clan surnames and evidence of {{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|大神神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine|qq=}} worship among the Keno lineage. He also suggests that while Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi-hime may not have been a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}, it is plausible that her lineage connected with the maternal ancestry of {{ill|Mimorowake|ja|御諸別命|Mimorowake=Mimorowake|lt=Omimorowake no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}, who was linked to both the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|毛野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ki no Kuni clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. Supporting evidence includes the prominence of thunder deity worship in the Keno regions, such as at {{ill|Kaminari Shrines|ja|雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminari Shrines|qq=}}.<ref>{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshio Hōga|qq=}}, "2. The Early Stages of the Keno Clan: Issues Surrounding the Emergence of the Keno Lineage," in *Studies of Ancient Clans Vol. 17: The Keno Clan – The Origins of the Eastern Province's Prominent Lineage*, Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 55–77.</ref>
== See also == * {{ill|List of figures before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of figures before the Asuka period|qq=}} * [[Kamitsukeno clan]] * [[Shimotsukeno clan]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox royalty | name = Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period|古墳時代]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Tōtsu Ayume Mememiwashi Hime | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | religious_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | allegiance = | clan = | father= {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔|1=Arakawato Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawatobime|qq=}} | siblings = {{ill|Kushitama no Mikoto|ja|久志多麻命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushitama no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Satsunanaho no Mikoto|ja|薩佐奈胡命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satsunanaho no Mikoto|qq=}} | issue = {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}} | special_notes = }} '''Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime''' (遠津年魚眼眼妙媛, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient figure in [[Japan]].
== Genealogy ==
Her father was {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔|1=Arakawato Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawatobime|qq=}}, a member of the {{ill|Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikoku no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan. She became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}, bearing the children {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}.
== Scholarly Analysis ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} and other sources, Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime was listed as a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}. She bore Prince {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, whose royal succession eligibility was compared to his half-brother, Ikume no Mikoto, later known as {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Suinin|qq=}}. However, it has been argued that the {{ill|Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikoku no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan traditionally did not intermarry with the imperial family, raising questions about the historicity of this claim.<ref>{{ill|Hōga Toshio|ja|宝賀寿男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōga Toshio|qq=}}, "The Early Stages of the Keno Clan," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII: The Keno Clan,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 31-33.</ref>
This theory posits that the {{ill|Keno clan|ja|毛野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|qq=}} may have shared ancestry with the {{ill|Omononushi|lt=Miwa clan|ja|三輪君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omononushi|qq=}}, evidenced by shared elements in naming conventions and religious practices, such as the worship of {{ill|Miwa deity|ja|三輪神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa deity|qq=}} and its widespread association with the {{ill|Raiden Shrine|ja|雷電神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raiden Shrine|qq=}} network in the region. It has been suggested that the maternal lineage of {{ill|Omorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別命|1=Mimorowake|lt=Mimorowake-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omorowake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}, a descendant of the Keno clan, included a daughter of Arakawatobime.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "The Role of Keno and Related Clans in Ancient Japanese Society," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 55-77.</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of people before the Asuka period]] * {{ill|Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}
