==Merged second translation== {{Infobox noble | name = Tōtsu Ayumemaguwashi Hime | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | birth_name = | other_names = Tōtsu Ayumememawashihime no Mikoto | posthumous_name = | deity_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_Rank = | allegiance = | family = | parents = Father: {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔|1=Arakawato Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawatobime|qq=}} | siblings = [[Kushitama no Mikoto]], [[Satsusana no Mikoto]] | children = [[Toyokuni Iribiko no Mikoto]], [[Toyokuniiri Hime no Mikoto]] | notes = | Name = Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi-hime | image = | image size = | Caption = | Era = {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} | Birth_date = Unknown | Death_date = Unknown | Renamed = | Other names = Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi-hime no Mikoto | Posthumous name = | Divine name = | Buddhist name = | Burial_place = | Court_Rank = | Master = | Clan = | Parents = Father: {{ill|Arakawato Hime|ja|荒河戸畔|1=Arakawato Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawato Hime|qq=}} | Siblings = {{ill|Kushitama no Mikoto|ja|久志多麻命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushitama no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Sasazano no Mikoto|ja|薩佐奈胡命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasazano no Mikoto|qq=}} | Children = {{ill|Toyokiiri Hiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiri Hiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyokumori Hime no Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumori Hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}} | Notes = }}
'''Tōtsu Ayumemaguwashi Hime''' (遠津年魚眼眼妙媛, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient Japanese figure from the [[Kofun period]]. '''Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi-hime''' (遠津年魚眼眼妙媛, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient {{ill|Japan|ja|日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan|qq=}}ese figure from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}.
== Genealogy == She was the daughter of {{ill|Arakawato Hime|ja|荒河戸畔|1=Arakawato Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawato Hime|qq=}}, a member of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. She became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}} and gave birth to {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}.
== Historical Analysis == In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} and other sources, Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi-hime is described as a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}, whose son, {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, is compared with his half-brother {{ill|Ikume Iribiko Isachi|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikume Iribiko Isachi|qq=}} in discussions of imperial succession. However, scholars note that it was not customary for women of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} to enter the emperor's harem. Additionally, Ikume no Mikoto's mother, {{ill|Mimaki-hime|ja|御間城姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimaki-hime|qq=}}, was an empress, making such a comparison unlikely.<ref>{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshio Hōga|qq=}}, "2. The Early Stages of the Keno Clan: Issues Surrounding the Emergence of the Keno Lineage," in *Studies of Ancient Clans Vol. 17: The Keno Clan – The Origins of the Eastern Province's Prominent Lineage*, Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 31–33.</ref>
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, Tōtsu Ayumemaguwashi Hime was one of the consorts of [[Emperor Sujin]]. She is credited with giving birth to {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, who is mentioned in a narrative comparing his potential for succession against his half-brother, {{ill|Ikume Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|活目尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikume Iribiko no Mikoto|qq=}} (later [[Emperor Suinin]]).
However, scholars question the credibility of this account. It has been noted that the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan did not traditionally intermarry with the imperial family, and succession comparisons with Emperor Suinin, whose mother was Empress {{ill|Mimaki-hime|ja|御間城姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimaki-hime|qq=}}, would not have been appropriate.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Kōdai Shizoku no Kenkyū XVII: Keno-shi Higashi no Yūzoku Shobuke no Genryū|author=Toshio Hōga|publisher=Aogaki Shuppan|year=2021|pages=31–33}}</ref>
Some scholars argue that the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Keno clan|ja|毛野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|qq=}}, which included the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, was closely related to the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa clan|qq=}}. The {{ill|Miwa deity|ja|三輪神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa deity|qq=}} and associated rituals are prominent within the Keno clan's lineage, and similarities in surnames suggest a shared ancestry.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Kōdai Shizoku no Kenkyū XVII: Keno-shi Higashi no Yūzoku Shobuke no Genryū|author=Toshio Hōga|publisher=Aogaki Shuppan|year=2021|pages=55–77}}</ref>
Despite doubts about her role as an imperial consort, connections between the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa clan|qq=}} have been suggested, particularly through marriage alliances. Additionally, the presence of numerous shrines dedicated to {{ill|Raijin|ja|雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|qq=}}—a deity associated with the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ki clan|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|qq=}}—in regions linked to the Keno clan further supports the hypothesis that Tōtsu Ayumemaguwashi Hime may have maternal ties to these groups.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Kōdai Shizoku no Kenkyū XVII: Keno-shi Higashi no Yūzoku Shobuke no Genryū|author=Toshio Hōga|publisher=Aogaki Shuppan|year=2021|pages=55–77}}</ref>
{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshio Hōga|qq=}} argues that the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Keno clan|ja|毛野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|qq=}} (later associated with the provinces of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzuke Province|qq=}} and {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Province|qq=}}) may have been related to the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa clan|qq=}}, citing similarities in clan surnames and evidence of {{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|大神神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine|qq=}} worship among the Keno lineage. He also suggests that while Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi-hime may not have been a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}, it is plausible that her lineage connected with the maternal ancestry of {{ill|Mimorowake|ja|御諸別命|Mimorowake=Mimorowake|lt=Omimorowake no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}, who was linked to both the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Keno clan|ja|毛野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ki no Kuni clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. Supporting evidence includes the prominence of thunder deity worship in the Keno regions, such as at {{ill|Kaminari Shrines|ja|雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminari Shrines|qq=}}.<ref>{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshio Hōga|qq=}}, "2. The Early Stages of the Keno Clan: Issues Surrounding the Emergence of the Keno Lineage," in *Studies of Ancient Clans Vol. 17: The Keno Clan – The Origins of the Eastern Province's Prominent Lineage*, Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 55–77.</ref>

{{Infobox royalty | name = Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period|古墳時代]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Tōtsu Ayume Mememiwashi Hime | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | religious_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | allegiance = | clan = | father= {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔|1=Arakawato Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawatobime|qq=}} | siblings = {{ill|Kushitama no Mikoto|ja|久志多麻命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushitama no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Satsunanaho no Mikoto|ja|薩佐奈胡命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satsunanaho no Mikoto|qq=}} | issue = {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}} | special_notes = }} '''Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime''' (遠津年魚眼眼妙媛, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient figure in [[Japan]].
== Genealogy ==
Her father was {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔|1=Arakawato Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawatobime|qq=}}, a member of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikoku no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan. She became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}, bearing the children {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}.
== Scholarly Analysis ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} and other sources, Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime was listed as a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}. She bore Prince {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, whose royal succession eligibility was compared to his half-brother, Ikume no Mikoto, later known as {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Suinin|qq=}}. However, it has been argued that the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikoku no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan traditionally did not intermarry with the imperial family, raising questions about the historicity of this claim.<ref>{{ill|Hōga Toshio|ja|宝賀寿男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōga Toshio|qq=}}, "The Early Stages of the Keno Clan," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII: The Keno Clan,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 31-33.</ref>
This theory posits that the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Keno clan|ja|毛野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|qq=}} may have shared ancestry with the {{ill|Omononushi|lt=Miwa clan|ja|三輪君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omononushi|qq=}}, evidenced by shared elements in naming conventions and religious practices, such as the worship of {{ill|Miwa deity|ja|三輪神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa deity|qq=}} and its widespread association with the {{ill|Raiden Shrine|ja|雷電神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raiden Shrine|qq=}} network in the region. It has been suggested that the maternal lineage of {{ill|Omorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別命|1=Mimorowake|lt=Mimorowake-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omorowake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}, a descendant of the Keno clan, included a daughter of Arakawatobime.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "The Role of Keno and Related Clans in Ancient Japanese Society," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 55-77.</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of people before the Asuka period]] * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}

==Merged second translation== {{Infobox noble | name = Tōtsu Ayumemaguwashi Hime | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | birth_name = | other_names = Tōtsu Ayumememawashihime no Mikoto | posthumous_name = | deity_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_Rank = | allegiance = | family = | parents = Father: {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔|1=Arakawato Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawatobime|qq=}} | siblings = [[Kushitama no Mikoto]], [[Satsusana no Mikoto]] | children = [[Toyokuni Iribiko no Mikoto]], [[Toyokuniiri Hime no Mikoto]] | notes = | Name = Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi-hime | image = | image size = | Caption = | Era = {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} | Birth_date = Unknown | Death_date = Unknown | Renamed = | Other names = Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi-hime no Mikoto | Posthumous name = | Divine name = | Buddhist name = | Burial_place = | Court_Rank = | Master = | Clan = | Parents = Father: {{ill|Arakawato Hime|ja|荒河戸畔|1=Arakawato Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawato Hime|qq=}} | Siblings = {{ill|Kushitama no Mikoto|ja|久志多麻命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushitama no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Sasazano no Mikoto|ja|薩佐奈胡命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasazano no Mikoto|qq=}} | Children = {{ill|Toyokiiri Hiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiri Hiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyokumori Hime no Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumori Hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}} | Notes = }}
'''Tōtsu Ayumemaguwashi Hime''' (遠津年魚眼眼妙媛, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient Japanese figure from the [[Kofun period]]. '''Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi-hime''' (遠津年魚眼眼妙媛, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient {{ill|Japan|ja|日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan|qq=}}ese figure from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}.
== Genealogy == She was the daughter of {{ill|Arakawato Hime|ja|荒河戸畔|1=Arakawato Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawato Hime|qq=}}, a member of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. She became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}} and gave birth to {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}.
== Historical Analysis == In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} and other sources, Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi-hime is described as a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}, whose son, {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, is compared with his half-brother {{ill|Ikume Iribiko Isachi|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikume Iribiko Isachi|qq=}} in discussions of imperial succession. However, scholars note that it was not customary for women of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} to enter the emperor's harem. Additionally, Ikume no Mikoto's mother, {{ill|Mimaki-hime|ja|御間城姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimaki-hime|qq=}}, was an empress, making such a comparison unlikely.<ref>{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshio Hōga|qq=}}, "2. The Early Stages of the Keno Clan: Issues Surrounding the Emergence of the Keno Lineage," in *Studies of Ancient Clans Vol. 17: The Keno Clan – The Origins of the Eastern Province's Prominent Lineage*, Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 31–33.</ref>
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, Tōtsu Ayumemaguwashi Hime was one of the consorts of [[Emperor Sujin]]. She is credited with giving birth to {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, who is mentioned in a narrative comparing his potential for succession against his half-brother, {{ill|Ikume Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|活目尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikume Iribiko no Mikoto|qq=}} (later [[Emperor Suinin]]).
However, scholars question the credibility of this account. It has been noted that the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan did not traditionally intermarry with the imperial family, and succession comparisons with Emperor Suinin, whose mother was Empress {{ill|Mimaki-hime|ja|御間城姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimaki-hime|qq=}}, would not have been appropriate.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Kōdai Shizoku no Kenkyū XVII: Keno-shi Higashi no Yūzoku Shobuke no Genryū|author=Toshio Hōga|publisher=Aogaki Shuppan|year=2021|pages=31–33}}</ref>
Some scholars argue that the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Keno clan|ja|毛野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|qq=}}, which included the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, was closely related to the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa clan|qq=}}. The {{ill|Miwa deity|ja|三輪神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa deity|qq=}} and associated rituals are prominent within the Keno clan's lineage, and similarities in surnames suggest a shared ancestry.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Kōdai Shizoku no Kenkyū XVII: Keno-shi Higashi no Yūzoku Shobuke no Genryū|author=Toshio Hōga|publisher=Aogaki Shuppan|year=2021|pages=55–77}}</ref>
Despite doubts about her role as an imperial consort, connections between the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa clan|qq=}} have been suggested, particularly through marriage alliances. Additionally, the presence of numerous shrines dedicated to {{ill|Raijin|ja|雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|qq=}}—a deity associated with the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ki clan|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|qq=}}—in regions linked to the Keno clan further supports the hypothesis that Tōtsu Ayumemaguwashi Hime may have maternal ties to these groups.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Kōdai Shizoku no Kenkyū XVII: Keno-shi Higashi no Yūzoku Shobuke no Genryū|author=Toshio Hōga|publisher=Aogaki Shuppan|year=2021|pages=55–77}}</ref>
{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshio Hōga|qq=}} argues that the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Keno clan|ja|毛野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|qq=}} (later associated with the provinces of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzuke Province|qq=}} and {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Province|qq=}}) may have been related to the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa clan|qq=}}, citing similarities in clan surnames and evidence of {{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|大神神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine|qq=}} worship among the Keno lineage. He also suggests that while Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi-hime may not have been a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}, it is plausible that her lineage connected with the maternal ancestry of {{ill|Mimorowake|ja|御諸別命|Mimorowake=Mimorowake|lt=Omimorowake no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}, who was linked to both the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Keno clan|ja|毛野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ki no Kuni clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. Supporting evidence includes the prominence of thunder deity worship in the Keno regions, such as at {{ill|Raijin Shrine|lt=Raijin Shrine|lt=Kaminari Shrines|ja|雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminari Shrines|qq=}}.<ref>{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshio Hōga|qq=}}, "2. The Early Stages of the Keno Clan: Issues Surrounding the Emergence of the Keno Lineage," in *Studies of Ancient Clans Vol. 17: The Keno Clan – The Origins of the Eastern Province's Prominent Lineage*, Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 55–77.</ref>

revision.text:
{{Infobox royalty | name = Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period|古墳時代]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Tōtsu Ayume Mememiwashi Hime | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | religious_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | allegiance = | clan = | father= {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔|1=Arakawato Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawatobime|qq=}} | siblings = {{ill|Kushitama no Mikoto|ja|久志多麻命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushitama no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Satsunanaho no Mikoto|ja|薩佐奈胡命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satsunanaho no Mikoto|qq=}} | issue = {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}} | special_notes = }} '''Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime''' (遠津年魚眼眼妙媛, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient figure in [[Japan]].
== Genealogy ==
Her father was {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔|1=Arakawato Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawatobime|qq=}}, a member of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikoku no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan. She became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}, bearing the children {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}.
== Scholarly Analysis ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} and other sources, Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime was listed as a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}. She bore Prince {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, whose royal succession eligibility was compared to his half-brother, Ikume no Mikoto, later known as {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Suinin|qq=}}. However, it has been argued that the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikoku no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan traditionally did not intermarry with the imperial family, raising questions about the historicity of this claim.<ref>{{ill|Toshio Hōga|lt=Toshio Hōga|lt=Hōga Toshio|ja|宝賀寿男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōga Toshio|qq=}}, "The Early Stages of the Keno Clan," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII: The Keno Clan,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 31-33.</ref>
This theory posits that the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Keno clan|ja|毛野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|qq=}} may have shared ancestry with the {{ill|Omononushi|lt=Miwa clan|ja|三輪君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omononushi|qq=}}, evidenced by shared elements in naming conventions and religious practices, such as the worship of {{ill|Miwa deity|ja|三輪神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa deity|qq=}} and its widespread association with the {{ill|Raiden Shrine|ja|雷電神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raiden Shrine|qq=}} network in the region. It has been suggested that the maternal lineage of {{ill|Omorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別命|1=Mimorowake|lt=Mimorowake-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omorowake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}, a descendant of the Keno clan, included a daughter of Arakawatobime.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "The Role of Keno and Related Clans in Ancient Japanese Society," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 55-77.</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of people before the Asuka period]] * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}

==Merged second translation== {{Infobox noble | name = Tōtsu Ayumemaguwashi Hime | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | birth_name = | other_names = Tōtsu Ayumememawashihime no Mikoto | posthumous_name = | deity_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_Rank = | allegiance = | family = | parents = Father: {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔|1=Arakawato Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawatobime|qq=}} | siblings = [[Kushitama no Mikoto]], [[Satsusana no Mikoto]] | children = [[Toyokuni Iribiko no Mikoto]], [[Toyokuniiri Hime no Mikoto]] | notes = | Name = Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi-hime | image = | image size = | Caption = | Era = {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} | Birth_date = Unknown | Death_date = Unknown | Renamed = | Other names = Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi-hime no Mikoto | Posthumous name = | Divine name = | Buddhist name = | Burial_place = | Court_Rank = | Master = | Clan = | Parents = Father: {{ill|Arakawato Hime|ja|荒河戸畔|1=Arakawato Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawato Hime|qq=}} | Siblings = {{ill|Kushitama no Mikoto|ja|久志多麻命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushitama no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Sasazano no Mikoto|ja|薩佐奈胡命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasazano no Mikoto|qq=}} | Children = {{ill|Toyokiiri Hiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiri Hiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyokumori Hime no Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumori Hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}} | Notes = }}
'''Tōtsu Ayumemaguwashi Hime''' (遠津年魚眼眼妙媛, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient Japanese figure from the [[Kofun period]]. '''Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi-hime''' (遠津年魚眼眼妙媛, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient {{ill|Japan|ja|日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan|qq=}}ese figure from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}.
== Genealogy == She was the daughter of {{ill|Arakawato Hime|ja|荒河戸畔|1=Arakawato Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawato Hime|qq=}}, a member of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. She became a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}} and gave birth to {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}.
== Historical Analysis == In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} and other sources, Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi-hime is described as a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}, whose son, {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, is compared with his half-brother {{ill|Ikume Iribiko Isachi|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikume Iribiko Isachi|qq=}} in discussions of imperial succession. However, scholars note that it was not customary for women of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} to enter the emperor's harem. Additionally, Ikume no Mikoto's mother, {{ill|Mimaki-hime|ja|御間城姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimaki-hime|qq=}}, was an empress, making such a comparison unlikely.<ref>{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshio Hōga|qq=}}, "2. The Early Stages of the Keno Clan: Issues Surrounding the Emergence of the Keno Lineage," in *Studies of Ancient Clans Vol. 17: The Keno Clan – The Origins of the Eastern Province's Prominent Lineage*, Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 31–33.</ref>
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, Tōtsu Ayumemaguwashi Hime was one of the consorts of [[Emperor Sujin]]. She is credited with giving birth to {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, who is mentioned in a narrative comparing his potential for succession against his half-brother, {{ill|Ikume Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|活目尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikume Iribiko no Mikoto|qq=}} (later [[Emperor Suinin]]).
However, scholars question the credibility of this account. It has been noted that the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan did not traditionally intermarry with the imperial family, and succession comparisons with Emperor Suinin, whose mother was Empress {{ill|Mimaki-hime|ja|御間城姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimaki-hime|qq=}}, would not have been appropriate.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Kōdai Shizoku no Kenkyū XVII: Keno-shi Higashi no Yūzoku Shobuke no Genryū|author=Toshio Hōga|publisher=Aogaki Shuppan|year=2021|pages=31–33}}</ref>
Some scholars argue that the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Keno clan|ja|毛野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|qq=}}, which included the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, was closely related to the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa clan|qq=}}. The {{ill|Miwa deity|ja|三輪神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa deity|qq=}} and associated rituals are prominent within the Keno clan's lineage, and similarities in surnames suggest a shared ancestry.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Kōdai Shizoku no Kenkyū XVII: Keno-shi Higashi no Yūzoku Shobuke no Genryū|author=Toshio Hōga|publisher=Aogaki Shuppan|year=2021|pages=55–77}}</ref>
Despite doubts about her role as an imperial consort, connections between the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa clan|qq=}} have been suggested, particularly through marriage alliances. Additionally, the presence of numerous shrines dedicated to {{ill|Raijin|ja|雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|qq=}}—a deity associated with the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ki clan|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|qq=}}—in regions linked to the Keno clan further supports the hypothesis that Tōtsu Ayumemaguwashi Hime may have maternal ties to these groups.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Kōdai Shizoku no Kenkyū XVII: Keno-shi Higashi no Yūzoku Shobuke no Genryū|author=Toshio Hōga|publisher=Aogaki Shuppan|year=2021|pages=55–77}}</ref>
{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshio Hōga|qq=}} argues that the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Keno clan|ja|毛野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|qq=}} (later associated with the provinces of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzuke Province|qq=}} and {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Province|qq=}}) may have been related to the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa clan|qq=}}, citing similarities in clan surnames and evidence of {{ill|Miwa Shrine|ja|大神神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine|qq=}} worship among the Keno lineage. He also suggests that while Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi-hime may not have been a consort of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}, it is plausible that her lineage connected with the maternal ancestry of {{ill|Mimorowake|ja|御諸別命|Mimorowake=Mimorowake|lt=Omimorowake no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}, who was linked to both the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Keno clan|ja|毛野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ki no Kuni clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. Supporting evidence includes the prominence of thunder deity worship in the Keno regions, such as at {{ill|Raijin Shrine|lt=Raijin Shrine|lt=Kaminari Shrines|ja|雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminari Shrines|qq=}}.<ref>{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshio Hōga|qq=}}, "2. The Early Stages of the Keno Clan: Issues Surrounding the Emergence of the Keno Lineage," in *Studies of Ancient Clans Vol. 17: The Keno Clan – The Origins of the Eastern Province's Prominent Lineage*, Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 55–77.</ref>
== See also == * {{ill|List of figures before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of figures before the Asuka period|qq=}} * [[Kamitsukeno clan]] * [[Shimotsukeno clan]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox royalty | name = Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period|古墳時代]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Tōtsu Ayume Mememiwashi Hime | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | religious_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | allegiance = | clan = | father= {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔|1=Arakawato Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawatobime|qq=}} | siblings = {{ill|Kushitama no Mikoto|ja|久志多麻命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushitama no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Satsunanaho no Mikoto|ja|薩佐奈胡命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satsunanaho no Mikoto|qq=}} | issue = {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}} | special_notes = }} '''Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime''' (遠津年魚眼眼妙媛, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient figure in [[Japan]].
== Genealogy ==
Her father was {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔|1=Arakawato Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawatobime|qq=}}, a member of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikoku no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan. She became a consort of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], bearing the children {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}.
== Scholarly Analysis ==
According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and other sources, Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime was listed as a consort of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. She bore Prince {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, whose royal succession eligibility was compared to his half-brother, Ikume no Mikoto, later known as [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]. However, it has been argued that the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikoku no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan traditionally did not intermarry with the imperial family, raising questions about the historicity of this claim.<ref>{{ill|Toshio Hōga|lt=Toshio Hōga|lt=Hōga Toshio|ja|宝賀寿男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōga Toshio|qq=}}, "The Early Stages of the Keno Clan," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII: The Keno Clan,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 31-33.</ref>
This theory posits that the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Keno clan|ja|毛野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|qq=}} may have shared ancestry with the {{ill|Omononushi|lt=Miwa clan|ja|三輪君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omononushi|qq=}}, evidenced by shared elements in naming conventions and religious practices, such as the worship of {{ill|Miwa deity|ja|三輪神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa deity|qq=}} and its widespread association with the {{ill|Raiden Shrine|ja|雷電神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raiden Shrine|qq=}} network in the region. It has been suggested that the maternal lineage of {{ill|Omorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別命|1=Mimorowake|lt=Mimorowake-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omorowake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}, a descendant of the Keno clan, included a daughter of Arakawatobime.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "The Role of Keno and Related Clans in Ancient Japanese Society," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 55-77.</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of people before the Asuka period]] * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}

==Merged second translation== {{Infobox noble | name = Tōtsu Ayumemaguwashi Hime | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | birth_name = | other_names = Tōtsu Ayumememawashihime no Mikoto | posthumous_name = | deity_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_Rank = | allegiance = | family = | parents = Father: {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔|1=Arakawato Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawatobime|qq=}} | siblings = [[Kushitama no Mikoto]], [[Satsusana no Mikoto]] | children = [[Toyokuni Iribiko no Mikoto]], [[Toyokuniiri Hime no Mikoto]] | notes = | Name = Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi-hime | image = | image size = | Caption = | Era = [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] | Birth_date = Unknown | Death_date = Unknown | Renamed = | Other names = Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi-hime no Mikoto | Posthumous name = | Divine name = | Buddhist name = | Burial_place = | Court_Rank = | Master = | Clan = | Parents = Father: {{ill|Arakawato Hime|ja|荒河戸畔|1=Arakawato Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawato Hime|qq=}} | Siblings = {{ill|Kushitama no Mikoto|ja|久志多麻命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushitama no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Sasazano no Mikoto|ja|薩佐奈胡命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasazano no Mikoto|qq=}} | Children = {{ill|Toyokiiri Hiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiri Hiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyokumori Hime no Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumori Hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}} | Notes = }}
'''Tōtsu Ayumemaguwashi Hime''' (遠津年魚眼眼妙媛, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient Japanese figure from the [[Kofun period]]. '''Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi-hime''' (遠津年魚眼眼妙媛, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]]ese figure from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
== Genealogy == She was the daughter of {{ill|Arakawato Hime|ja|荒河戸畔|1=Arakawato Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawato Hime|qq=}}, a member of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. She became a consort of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] and gave birth to {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}.
== Historical Analysis == In the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and other sources, Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi-hime is described as a consort of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], whose son, {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, is compared with his half-brother [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Ikume Iribiko Isachi]] in discussions of imperial succession. However, scholars note that it was not customary for women of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} to enter the emperor's harem. Additionally, Ikume no Mikoto's mother, [[:en:Mimaki-hime|Mimaki-hime]], was an empress, making such a comparison unlikely.<ref>{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshio Hōga|qq=}}, "2. The Early Stages of the Keno Clan: Issues Surrounding the Emergence of the Keno Lineage," in *Studies of Ancient Clans Vol. 17: The Keno Clan – The Origins of the Eastern Province's Prominent Lineage*, Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 31–33.</ref>
According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Tōtsu Ayumemaguwashi Hime was one of the consorts of [[Emperor Sujin]]. She is credited with giving birth to {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, who is mentioned in a narrative comparing his potential for succession against his half-brother, {{ill|Ikume Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|活目尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikume Iribiko no Mikoto|qq=}} (later [[Emperor Suinin]]).
However, scholars question the credibility of this account. It has been noted that the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan did not traditionally intermarry with the imperial family, and succession comparisons with Emperor Suinin, whose mother was Empress [[:en:Mimaki-hime|Mimaki-hime]], would not have been appropriate.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Kōdai Shizoku no Kenkyū XVII: Keno-shi Higashi no Yūzoku Shobuke no Genryū|author=Toshio Hōga|publisher=Aogaki Shuppan|year=2021|pages=31–33}}</ref>
Some scholars argue that the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Keno clan|ja|毛野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|qq=}}, which included the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, was closely related to the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa clan|qq=}}. The {{ill|Miwa deity|ja|三輪神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa deity|qq=}} and associated rituals are prominent within the Keno clan's lineage, and similarities in surnames suggest a shared ancestry.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Kōdai Shizoku no Kenkyū XVII: Keno-shi Higashi no Yūzoku Shobuke no Genryū|author=Toshio Hōga|publisher=Aogaki Shuppan|year=2021|pages=55–77}}</ref>
Despite doubts about her role as an imperial consort, connections between the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa clan|qq=}} have been suggested, particularly through marriage alliances. Additionally, the presence of numerous shrines dedicated to [[:en:Raijin|Raijin]]—a deity associated with the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ki clan|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|qq=}}—in regions linked to the Keno clan further supports the hypothesis that Tōtsu Ayumemaguwashi Hime may have maternal ties to these groups.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Kōdai Shizoku no Kenkyū XVII: Keno-shi Higashi no Yūzoku Shobuke no Genryū|author=Toshio Hōga|publisher=Aogaki Shuppan|year=2021|pages=55–77}}</ref>
{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshio Hōga|qq=}} argues that the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Keno clan|ja|毛野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|qq=}} (later associated with the provinces of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzuke Province|qq=}} and {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Province|qq=}}) may have been related to the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa clan|qq=}}, citing similarities in clan surnames and evidence of [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Miwa Shrine]] worship among the Keno lineage. He also suggests that while Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi-hime may not have been a consort of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], it is plausible that her lineage connected with the maternal ancestry of {{ill|Mimorowake|ja|御諸別命|Mimorowake=Mimorowake|lt=Omimorowake no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}, who was linked to both the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Keno clan|ja|毛野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ki no Kuni clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. Supporting evidence includes the prominence of thunder deity worship in the Keno regions, such as at {{ill|Raijin Shrine|lt=Raijin Shrine|lt=Kaminari Shrines|ja|雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminari Shrines|qq=}}.<ref>{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshio Hōga|qq=}}, "2. The Early Stages of the Keno Clan: Issues Surrounding the Emergence of the Keno Lineage," in *Studies of Ancient Clans Vol. 17: The Keno Clan – The Origins of the Eastern Province's Prominent Lineage*, Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 55–77.</ref>
== See also == * {{ill|List of figures before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of figures before the Asuka period|qq=}} * [[Kamitsukeno clan]] * [[Shimotsukeno clan]]

{{Infobox royalty | name = Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period|古墳時代]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Tōtsu Ayume Mememiwashi Hime | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | religious_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | allegiance = | clan = | father= {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔|1=Arakawato Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawatobime|qq=}} | siblings = {{ill|Kushitama no Mikoto|ja|久志多麻命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushitama no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Satsunanaho no Mikoto|ja|薩佐奈胡命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satsunanaho no Mikoto|qq=}} | issue = {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}} | special_notes = }} '''Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime''' (遠津年魚眼眼妙媛, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient figure in [[Japan]].
== Genealogy ==
Her father was {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔|1=Arakawato Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawatobime|qq=}}, a member of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikoku no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan. She became a consort of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], bearing the children {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}.
== Scholarly Analysis ==
According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and other sources, Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime was listed as a consort of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. She bore Prince {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, whose royal succession eligibility was compared to his half-brother, Ikume no Mikoto, later known as [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]. However, it has been argued that the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikoku no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan traditionally did not intermarry with the imperial family, raising questions about the historicity of this claim.<ref>{{ill|Toshio Hōga|lt=Toshio Hōga|lt=Hōga Toshio|ja|宝賀寿男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōga Toshio|qq=}}, "The Early Stages of the Keno Clan," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII: The Keno Clan,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 31-33.</ref>
This theory posits that the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Keno clan|ja|毛野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|qq=}} may have shared ancestry with the {{ill|Omononushi|lt=Miwa clan|ja|三輪君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omononushi|qq=}}, evidenced by shared elements in naming conventions and religious practices, such as the worship of {{ill|Miwa deity|ja|三輪神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa deity|qq=}} and its widespread association with the {{ill|Raiden Shrine|ja|雷電神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raiden Shrine|qq=}} network in the region. It has been suggested that the maternal lineage of {{ill|Omorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別命|1=Mimorowake|lt=Mimorowake-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omorowake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}, a descendant of the Keno clan, included a daughter of Arakawatobime.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "The Role of Keno and Related Clans in Ancient Japanese Society," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 55-77.</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of people before the Asuka period]] * {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Keno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}

==Merged second translation== {{Infobox noble | name = Tōtsu Ayumemaguwashi Hime | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | birth_name = | other_names = Tōtsu Ayumememawashihime no Mikoto | posthumous_name = | deity_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_Rank = | allegiance = | family = | parents = Father: {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔|1=Arakawato Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawatobime|qq=}} | siblings = [[Kushitama no Mikoto]], [[Satsusana no Mikoto]] | children = [[Toyokuni Iribiko no Mikoto]], [[Toyokuniiri Hime no Mikoto]] | notes = | Name = Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi-hime | image = | image size = | Caption = | Era = [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] | Birth_date = Unknown | Death_date = Unknown | Renamed = | Other names = Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi-hime no Mikoto | Posthumous name = | Divine name = | Buddhist name = | Burial_place = | Court_Rank = | Master = | Clan = | Parents = Father: {{ill|Arakawato Hime|ja|荒河戸畔|1=Arakawato Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawato Hime|qq=}} | Siblings = {{ill|Kushitama no Mikoto|ja|久志多麻命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushitama no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Sasazano no Mikoto|ja|薩佐奈胡命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasazano no Mikoto|qq=}} | Children = {{ill|Toyokiiri Hiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiri Hiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyokumori Hime no Mikoto|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumori Hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}} | Notes = }}
'''Tōtsu Ayumemaguwashi Hime''' (遠津年魚眼眼妙媛, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient Japanese figure from the [[Kofun period]]. '''Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi-hime''' (遠津年魚眼眼妙媛, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]]ese figure from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
== Genealogy == She was the daughter of {{ill|Arakawato Hime|ja|荒河戸畔|1=Arakawato Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawato Hime|qq=}}, a member of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. She became a consort of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] and gave birth to {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}.
== Historical Analysis == In the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and other sources, Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi-hime is described as a consort of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], whose son, {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, is compared with his half-brother [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Ikume Iribiko Isachi]] in discussions of imperial succession. However, scholars note that it was not customary for women of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} to enter the emperor's harem. Additionally, Ikume no Mikoto's mother, [[:en:Mimaki-hime|Mimaki-hime]], was an empress, making such a comparison unlikely.<ref>{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshio Hōga|qq=}}, "2. The Early Stages of the Keno Clan: Issues Surrounding the Emergence of the Keno Lineage," in *Studies of Ancient Clans Vol. 17: The Keno Clan – The Origins of the Eastern Province's Prominent Lineage*, Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 31–33.</ref>
According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Tōtsu Ayumemaguwashi Hime was one of the consorts of [[Emperor Sujin]]. She is credited with giving birth to {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, who is mentioned in a narrative comparing his potential for succession against his half-brother, {{ill|Ikume Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|活目尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikume Iribiko no Mikoto|qq=}} (later [[Emperor Suinin]]).
However, scholars question the credibility of this account. It has been noted that the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan did not traditionally intermarry with the imperial family, and succession comparisons with Emperor Suinin, whose mother was Empress [[:en:Mimaki-hime|Mimaki-hime]], would not have been appropriate.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Kōdai Shizoku no Kenkyū XVII: Keno-shi Higashi no Yūzoku Shobuke no Genryū|author=Toshio Hōga|publisher=Aogaki Shuppan|year=2021|pages=31–33}}</ref>
Some scholars argue that the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Keno clan|ja|毛野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|qq=}}, which included the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, was closely related to the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa clan|qq=}}. The {{ill|Miwa deity|ja|三輪神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa deity|qq=}} and associated rituals are prominent within the Keno clan's lineage, and similarities in surnames suggest a shared ancestry.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Kōdai Shizoku no Kenkyū XVII: Keno-shi Higashi no Yūzoku Shobuke no Genryū|author=Toshio Hōga|publisher=Aogaki Shuppan|year=2021|pages=55–77}}</ref>
Despite doubts about her role as an imperial consort, connections between the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ki clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa clan|qq=}} have been suggested, particularly through marriage alliances. Additionally, the presence of numerous shrines dedicated to [[:en:Raijin|Raijin]]—a deity associated with the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ki clan|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki clan|qq=}}—in regions linked to the Keno clan further supports the hypothesis that Tōtsu Ayumemaguwashi Hime may have maternal ties to these groups.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Kōdai Shizoku no Kenkyū XVII: Keno-shi Higashi no Yūzoku Shobuke no Genryū|author=Toshio Hōga|publisher=Aogaki Shuppan|year=2021|pages=55–77}}</ref>
{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshio Hōga|qq=}} argues that the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Keno clan|ja|毛野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|qq=}} (later associated with the provinces of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzuke Province|qq=}} and {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Province|qq=}}) may have been related to the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa clan|qq=}}, citing similarities in clan surnames and evidence of [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Miwa Shrine]] worship among the Keno lineage. He also suggests that while Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi-hime may not have been a consort of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], it is plausible that her lineage connected with the maternal ancestry of {{ill|Mimorowake|ja|御諸別命|Mimorowake=Mimorowake|lt=Omimorowake no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}, who was linked to both the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Keno clan|ja|毛野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ki no Kuni clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. Supporting evidence includes the prominence of thunder deity worship in the Keno regions, such as at {{ill|Raijin Shrine|lt=Raijin Shrine|lt=Kaminari Shrines|ja|雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminari Shrines|qq=}}.<ref>{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshio Hōga|qq=}}, "2. The Early Stages of the Keno Clan: Issues Surrounding the Emergence of the Keno Lineage," in *Studies of Ancient Clans Vol. 17: The Keno Clan – The Origins of the Eastern Province's Prominent Lineage*, Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 55–77.</ref>
== See also == * {{ill|List of figures before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of figures before the Asuka period|qq=}} * [[Kamitsukeno clan]] * [[Shimotsukeno clan]]

{{Infobox royalty | name = Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period|古墳時代]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Tōtsu Ayume Mememiwashi Hime | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | religious_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | allegiance = | clan = | father= {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔|1=Arakawato Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawatobime|qq=}} | siblings = {{ill|Kushitama no Mikoto|ja|久志多麻命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushitama no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Satsunanaho no Mikoto|ja|薩佐奈胡命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satsunanaho no Mikoto|qq=}} | issue = {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}} | special_notes = }} '''Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime''' (遠津年魚眼眼妙媛, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient figure in [[Japan]].
== Genealogy ==
Her father was {{ill|Arakawatobime|ja|荒河戸畔|1=Arakawato Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawatobime|qq=}}, a member of the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikoku no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan. She became a consort of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], bearing the children {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}.
== Scholarly Analysis ==
According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and other sources, Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime was listed as a consort of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. She bore Prince {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, whose royal succession eligibility was compared to his half-brother, Ikume no Mikoto, later known as [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]. However, it has been argued that the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kikoku no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikoku no Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan traditionally did not intermarry with the imperial family, raising questions about the historicity of this claim.<ref>{{ill|Toshio Hōga|lt=Toshio Hōga|lt=Hōga Toshio|ja|宝賀寿男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōga Toshio|qq=}}, "The Early Stages of the Keno Clan," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII: The Keno Clan,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 31-33.</ref>
This theory posits that the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Kamitsukeno clan|lt=Keno clan|ja|毛野氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno clan|qq=}} may have shared ancestry with the {{ill|Omononushi|lt=Miwa clan|ja|三輪君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omononushi|qq=}}, evidenced by shared elements in naming conventions and religious practices, such as the worship of {{ill|Miwa deity|ja|三輪神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa deity|qq=}} and its widespread association with the {{ill|Raiden Shrine|ja|雷電神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raiden Shrine|qq=}} network in the region. It has been suggested that the maternal lineage of {{ill|Omorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別命|1=Mimorowake|lt=Mimorowake-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omorowake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}, a descendant of the Keno clan, included a daughter of Arakawatobime.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "The Role of Keno and Related Clans in Ancient Japanese Society," in ''Studies on Ancient Clans XVII,'' Aogaki Publishing, 2021, pp. 55-77.</ref>

[[sn:Totsu Ayumemaguwashihime]] [[sn:otsu Ayumemaguwashihime]] [[ja:遠津年魚眼眼妙媛]]
